description: Classify medical literature sentences as ADE (Adverse Drug Event) or NOT_ADE. Cortex v1.0 uses biologically-grounded cortical column architecture with BCM plasticity, critical period dynamics, and competitive routing. Fixes all failure modes diagnosed in v3.04-enterprise.

route_definitions.name: causation
route_definitions.focus: Direct causal language drug to harm

route_definitions.name: context
route_definitions.focus: Therapeutic intent vs documented adverse outcome

seed_nodes.description: Primary cortical column — general ADE classifier, fires when no specialist matches (specificity=0)
seed_nodes.prompt:
You are an expert pharmacovigilance classifier trained to identify Adverse Drug Events (ADEs) in medical literature.

An Adverse Drug Event (ADE) is an unintended, harmful outcome caused by or associated with a drug — including toxicity, organ damage, hypersensitivity reactions, and unexpected side effects documented in a patient.

NOT an ADE: desired therapeutic effects (tumor shrinkage, pain relief, infection clearance), negated adverse outcomes ('did not develop rash'), mechanism-of-action descriptions without patient harm, epidemiological association statements, laboratory findings without documented patient harm.

Classify the following sentence as ADE or NOT_ADE.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"classification": "ADE" or "NOT_ADE", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}

seed_nodes.description: Negation specialist column — sentences with explicit negation language are most common false positive source (specificity=1)
seed_nodes.prompt:
You are a negation specialist in pharmacovigilance NLP. This sentence contains negation language and must be classified carefully.

Key principle — negation scope determines classification:
  'No relief from pain' negates relief — NOT an ADE indicator. Classify NOT_ADE.
  'Did not develop nephrotoxicity' negates the ADE — classify NOT_ADE.
  'Rash did not resolve despite treatment' — rash IS present — classify ADE.
  'No evidence of hepatotoxicity' — outcome absent — classify NOT_ADE.

Classify as ADE only if an adverse event is AFFIRMED in the sentence despite negation of something else.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"classification": "ADE" or "NOT_ADE", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}

seed_nodes.description: Causation specialist column — sentences with explicit causal language (induced, associated) are high-confidence ADE patterns (specificity=1)
seed_nodes.prompt:
You are a causation specialist in pharmacovigilance. This sentence contains direct causal language ('induced' or 'associated with').

Your task: confirm whether this causal language links a specific drug to a documented adverse outcome in a patient.

True causation — classify ADE:
  'Drug X induced nephrotoxicity' — direct drug-to-harm attribution.
  'Cisplatin-induced peripheral neuropathy was observed.'

Not causation — classify NOT_ADE:
  'Associated with improved outcomes' — beneficial, not adverse.
  'Fever associated with infection, not drug' — cause is non-drug.
  'Mechanisms associated with drug resistance' — no patient harm.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"classification": "ADE" or "NOT_ADE", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}

seed_nodes.description: Short-sentence specialist column — telegraphic clinical notations without negation are often high-confidence ADE reports (specificity=2)
seed_nodes.prompt:
You are a clinical notation specialist in pharmacovigilance. This is a short sentence (under 80 chars) without negation — typical of telegraphic clinical notes or case report abstracts.

Short non-negated sentences in medical literature often represent condensed, high-confidence ADE reports:
  'Amoxicillin rash.' — ADE
  'Vancomycin-induced nephrotoxicity.' — ADE
  'Hepatotoxicity from methotrexate.' — ADE

But can also be incomplete or non-ADE:
  'Metformin prescribed for diabetes.' — therapeutic, NOT_ADE
  'Drug resistance to penicillin.' — mechanism, NOT_ADE

Classify based on whether this short sentence unambiguously links a drug to an adverse outcome.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"classification": "ADE" or "NOT_ADE", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}

v3.04_failure_modes: FM-1 Routing dilution: fixed by winner-take-all competitive routing (specificity-based)
FM-2 Route error NOT_ADE voting: fixed by route abstention (None return, excluded from ensemble)
FM-3 MCQ complexity harm: fixed by BCM-gated rehearsal weight (immature columns get less)
FM-4 Small eval pool: fixed (200 cases, was 100)
FM-5 Trigger overlap: fixed by Jaccard audit before columnar genesis (max=0.50)
biological_mechanisms: BCM theory (Bienenstock, Cooper, Munro 1982): sliding modification threshold
Critical period plasticity (Wiesel & Hubel 1963; Hensch 2004): rising genesis threshold
Neurogenesis & apoptosis (Eriksson 1998): columnar genesis and pruning
Engram theory (Semon 1904; Josselyn 2015): MemoryTrace bequeathal on pruning
Predictive coding (Rao & Ballard 1999): classification as prediction, errors drive learning
